title: Power and design analysis
text: classic case closed-form analytic power, simulation matches. roughly 50% power, Type M ratio well 1. Conditional significance, estimated effect exaggerated, even though average estimate replicates unbiased. reflects statistical-significance filter, precisely design analysis meant expose.
code: spec <- build_spec(list(   name = "two_group", seed = 1, design_kind = "between", n_subject = 64,   factor_name = "group", lev1 = "control", lev2 = "treatment",   intercept = 100, effect = 5, family = "gaussian", resp_name = "score", sigma = 10))  pw <- power_design(spec, n_sims = 500) unlist(pw[c("power", "type_s", "type_m", "true_effect", "mean_estimate")]) #>         power        type_s        type_m   true_effect mean_estimate  #>      0.504000      0.000000      1.372571      5.000000      4.947259

R interface fits maximal model y ~ cond + (1 + cond | subject) + (1 + cond | item) lme4/lmerTest tests fixed effect Satterthwaite degrees freedom. n_converged reports many replicates maximal model actually fit. useful diagnostic right, since convergence problems common small crossed designs, denominator power, significant proportion among converged replicates. power_mixed() runs pilotr’s simulation loop portable specification rather wrapping existing power package. covers territory pioneered simr (Green MacLeod, 2016) mixedpower (Kumle, Vo Draschkow, 2021). pilotr differs driven cross-language specification, reporting Type S Type M errors alongside power, built-parallelisation.
code: spec_c <- build_spec(list(   name = "priming", seed = 1, design_kind = "within", include_items = TRUE,   n_subject = 24, n_item = 18,   factor_name = "condition", lev1 = "related", lev2 = "unrelated",   intercept = 6, effect = 0.06,   subj_int_sd = 0.12, subj_slope_sd = 0.04, subj_corr = 0.2,   item_int_sd = 0.08, item_slope_sd = 0.02, item_corr = -0.1,   family = "shifted_lognormal", resp_name = "RT", sigma = 0.3, shift = 200)) pm <- power_mixed(spec_c, n_sims = 20)   # tiny for the vignette; use >= 200 in practice unlist(pm[c("power", "type_s", "type_m", "n_converged")]) #>       power      type_s      type_m n_converged  #>    0.800000    0.000000    1.193659   20.000000

title: Power and design analysis
text: following sweep number subjects shows power crosses target.  shaded band shows Monte Carlo interval, binomial standard error power estimate converged replicates widened 95% envelope.
code: curve <- power_curve_mixed(spec_c, subject_ns = c(8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 44, 56), n_sims = 50) curve #>   n_subject power   type_m n_converged #> 1         8  0.16 1.998682          50 #> 2        12  0.34 1.639186          50 #> 3        16  0.48 1.404611          50 #> 4        24  0.80 1.189207          50 #> 5        32  0.86 1.129062          50 #> 6        44  0.96 1.046713          50 #> 7        56  0.96 1.048449          50 # The transparent device canvas is what lets the page colour through: a ggplot theme alone # cannot do it, since the device paints white underneath. The website's dark mode then # inverts the figure's ink, so the axes and labels follow the theme instead of sitting on # an opaque matte. library(ggplot2) # Each power estimate is a proportion over the converged replicates, so it carries a # binomial Monte Carlo standard error. The shaded band is the 95% interval. curve$se <- sqrt(curve$power * (1 - curve$power) / curve$n_converged) ggplot(curve, aes(n_subject, power)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pmax(0, power - 1.96 * se), ymax = pmin(1, power + 1.96 * se)),               alpha = .15, fill = "#2C6FB0") +   geom_line(colour = "#2C6FB0", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(colour = "#2C6FB0", size = 2.6) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +   labs(x = expression(italic(N) ~ "subjects"), y = "Power") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   # theme_minimal still paints a white plot.background over the transparent canvas, so   # both surfaces have to be cleared for the page colour to reach the figure. The ink is   # left at its default: the website inverts the figure in dark mode, which turns the dark   # axis text light, whereas a fixed mid-grey would be inverted into a muddy tan.   theme(plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),         panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),         panel.grid       = element_line(colour = "grey80"))

title: Precision and ROPE design analysis
text: ROPE carried sweep. Leaving rope default compare interval region wide effect , decision probability fall N rather rise. N grows interval tightens p_meaningful rises, design reaches determinate decision reliably. Reading sweep smallest N meets target ‘p_meaningful ≥ 0.90’ puts design around 220 subjects. informative criterion power point null, asks whether study can distinguish effect negligible one rather merely zero. estimate rests 200 replicates, converged, Monte Carlo standard error 0.035, figure shows band. p_equivalent stays 0 throughout, correctly . true effect 0.05 lies outside ROPE construction, interval ever land entirely inside . column earns place designs practical equivalence hypothesis interest.  two panels answer question either side. left, dashed line marks 0.90 target sweep read . right marks width 0.06, confidence interval centred true effect just clears ROPE 0.02, since interval keep lower limit ROPE must narrower 2 × (0.05 − 0.02). Width falls line well decision probability reaches 0.90, interval centred exactly true effect best case sampling variation moves centre .
code: prc <- precision_curve(spec_c, focal = c(effect = 0.05),                        subject_ns = c(15, 30, 60, 100, 140, 180, 220, 260),                        rope = 0.02, n_sims = 200) prc[, c("n_subject", "p_meaningful", "p_equivalent", "mean_ci_width", "n_converged")] #>   n_subject p_meaningful p_equivalent mean_ci_width n_converged #> 1        15        0.140            0    0.12163610         200 #> 2        30        0.290            0    0.08462110         200 #> 3        60        0.525            0    0.05996175         200 #> 4       100        0.740            0    0.04732256         200 #> 5       140        0.845            0    0.04069929         200 #> 6       180        0.885            0    0.03678134         200 #> 7       220        0.925            0    0.03369284         200 #> 8       260        0.940            0    0.03166924         200 library(ggplot2) # Each probability is a proportion over the converged replicates, so it carries a binomial # Monte Carlo standard error, and the band is its 95% interval. The two panels are on # different scales, hence the free y axis. prc$se <- sqrt(prc$p_meaningful * (1 - prc$p_meaningful) / prc$n_converged) panels <- c("P(CI outside the ROPE)", "Mean 95% CI width") long <- rbind(   data.frame(n_subject = prc$n_subject, panel = panels[1], y = prc$p_meaningful,              lo = pmax(0, prc$p_meaningful - 1.96 * prc$se),              hi = pmin(1, prc$p_meaningful + 1.96 * prc$se)),   data.frame(n_subject = prc$n_subject, panel = panels[2], y = prc$mean_ci_width,              lo = NA, hi = NA)) long$panel <- factor(long$panel, levels = panels) # 0.90 is the target decision probability; 0.06 is the width at which a CI centred on the # true effect just clears the ROPE, that is 2 * (0.05 - 0.02). refs <- data.frame(panel = factor(panels, levels = panels), ref = c(0.90, 0.06))  ggplot(long, aes(n_subject, y)) +   geom_hline(data = refs, aes(yintercept = ref), linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = .15, fill = "#2C6FB0", na.rm = TRUE) +   geom_line(colour = "#2C6FB0", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(colour = "#2C6FB0", size = 2.2) +   facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = expression(italic(N) ~ "subjects"), y = NULL) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   # theme_minimal still paints a white plot.background over the transparent canvas, so both   # surfaces have to be cleared for the page colour to reach the figure.   theme(plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),         panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),         panel.grid       = element_line(colour = "grey80"),         strip.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA))

what: Simulation-based power and design analysis for a crossed mixed-effects design — power_mixed
title: Simulation-based power and design analysis for a crossed mixed-effects design — power_mixed
text: replicate, simulate ground-truth specification, fit maximal model y ~ cond + (1 + cond | subject) + (1 + cond | item) lmerTest, test fixed effect using Satterthwaite p-values. Reports power together Type S Type M errors Gelman Carlin (2014). Requires lme4 lmerTest packages.

title: Simulation-based power and design analysis for a crossed mixed-effects design — power_mixed

title: Simulation-based power and design analysis for a crossed mixed-effects design — power_mixed
text: spec design specification (path list) exactly one within-unit factor crossed design item unit. n_sims Number Monte Carlo replicates. power estimate carries Monte Carlo standard error sqrt(p * (1 - p) / n_sims), type_s type_m average significant replicates alone, settle slowly still. least 200 replicates advisable study planning. alpha Two-sided significance level. workers Number local worker processes spread replicates. default 1 runs serially. every replicate seeds shared RNG index, worker count returns results identical serial run. mixed-model fits dominate cost, speed-close linear number cores.
